Trading Education • 2/8/2026 • 7 min read

Trader Psychology: Managing Emotions

How to cope with emotions in trading: fear, greed, FOMO, and tilt. Practical techniques for maintaining control.

MakeTrades Team
MakeTrades Expert

Understanding Trader Emotions

Every trader faces the same psychological challenges regardless of experience level. Fear manifests as premature position closing or inability to pull the trigger on valid setups. Greed appears as overleveraging and refusing to take profits. Tilt, borrowed from poker terminology, describes the irrational decision-making that follows a series of unexpected losses or missed opportunities.

Practical Control Techniques

Pre-market routine: review your trading plan, identify key levels and potential setups, and set maximum loss limits before the session begins. During trading, use checklists for each trade entry to prevent impulsive decisions. After significant losses, implement a mandatory cooling-off period (minimum 30 minutes) before placing the next trade.

Long-Term Psychological Development

Treat trading as a probability game rather than a personal battle. Each individual trade outcome is meaningless; only the statistical edge over hundreds of trades matters. Journaling helps identify emotional patterns. Review your journal weekly to spot recurring psychological triggers and develop specific countermeasures for each one.

Tags: psychologyemotionstraderfeargreedtiltdiscipline
